text.title: Service Bootstrap Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: 8 testable gates every greenfield service MUST clear on day 0 before being declared "ready to ship." Skipping any gate guarantees a discovery during the first incident.

rule.statement: Repo MUST follow the project's canonical layout: `src/` (or framework default), `tests/`, `docs/`, `.github/workflows/`, `AGENTS.md` at root, `CLAUDE.md` at root pointing to `@AGENTS.md`. Ad-hoc layouts = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Layout drift compounds; the first 10 services set the template every subsequent service copies. Canonical layout on day 0 is the cheapest standardisation win.

rule.statement: CI MUST run on the first commit: at minimum lint + format + unit-test gates. CI files committed but disabled = REJECT. Green build on commit 1 is the proof.
rule.rationale: "We'll add CI later" never happens; by commit 50 the codebase is too noisy to enable lint without a week of cleanup.

rule.statement: Repo MUST contain `AGENTS.md` (20-80 lines, file table + key types + commands) AND `CLAUDE.md` containing exactly `@AGENTS.md`. Both files committed on day 0; empty placeholders = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Multi-agent workflows (Claude Code, Codex, etc.) require both files for orientation. Day-0 commit is the only time they get written; deferred = forgotten.

rule.statement: If the deploy target uses containers (k8s / Fly / Railway with Docker / etc.), a working `Dockerfile` MUST build and the image MUST start locally. If deploy target is serverless (Vercel / Cloudflare Workers), the build command MUST produce a deployable artefact locally. "Will containerize before launch" = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Dockerfile pain (multi-stage, image size, base image vulns) is a 1-day distraction at any time except day 0, when it's a 2-hour task with no other context.

rule.statement: Service MUST emit at minimum: a structured log line per request (level, request_id, route, status, latency), an error tracker SDK initialised (Sentry / Highlight / equivalent) with DSN from env, and a health endpoint (`/healthz` or platform default). All three stubs present = PASS.
rule.rationale: Observability wired in production-AFTER-incident costs hours during a real incident, on top of the incident itself. Stubs on day 0 cost ~15 minutes and pay off the first time a request 500s.

rule.statement: `.env.example` MUST exist listing required env vars (without values); `.env.local` and any real-secret file MUST be in `.gitignore`; a secret-scanner pre-commit hook (gitleaks / trufflehog) MUST be installed. Real secrets in any committed file = REJECT.
rule.rationale: First-commit secret leaks are the single highest-frequency security incident on greenfield repos. Pre-commit scanning is the only durable mitigation.

rule.statement: Repo MUST contain `CHANGELOG.md` with `## [Unreleased]` section AND a documented rollback procedure (either via `solo/dev/deploy-notes-template-with-rollback` or inline `ROLLBACK.md`). No rollback doc = REJECT — you cannot ship without a documented exit.
rule.rationale: Rollback procedure invented mid-incident usually doesn't work; documented day-0 rollback is the safety net that lets you ship fast later.

rule.statement: Bootstrap completion MUST emit `BOOTSTRAP.md` listing each gate, its pass evidence (filename / CI run / screenshot), and the operator's signature with date. Unsigned bootstraps are not "ready to ship" regardless of how green the repo looks.
rule.rationale: A signed record makes the bootstrap auditable later (post-mortems, compliance, onboarding new operators) and forces the operator to actually verify each gate.

example.title: BOOTSTRAP.md output
example.text: service: payments-api bootstrapped: 2026-05-20 operator: @nero gates: [layout=PASS(canonical), ci=PASS(run #1 green), agents_md=PASS(48 lines), container=PASS(Dockerfile builds 142MB), observability=PASS(structured logs + Sentry stub + /healthz), secrets=PASS(gitleaks installed, .env.example committed), release_notes=PASS(CHANGELOG + ROLLBACK.md), bootstrap_record=this file] signature: @nero 2026-05-20
